mechanism_narrative: Sustained upward drift in the Consumer Price Index over three months raises inflation expectations, compressing real yields and pushing nominal 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between CPIAUCSL 3-month change and 20-day forward yield change is below 0.10, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4% from its current level of 3.7%, a recession signal is triggered that drives safe-haven demand into Treasuries, lowering the 10-year yield over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In episodes where UNRATE exceeds 4%, the 20-day forward yield falls less than 50% of the time, or average yield change is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in housing starts and industrial production over two consecutive months signals a real-sector contraction that depresses long-run growth expectations, causing the 10-year yield to fall over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST monthly decline > 5% and INDPRO monthly decline > 0.5% in the same month
failure_condition: When both triggers are simultaneously active, the 20-day forward yield falls in less than 60% of cases, or the average yield change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary easing cycle, each 25bp cut in the federal funds rate signals worsening economic conditions that dominate the mechanical price effect, pushing 10-year yields lower over the next 20 trading days. During a tightening cycle, rising rates push yields higher. This mechanism's sign reverses with the regime.
failure_condition: During easing cycles (FEDFUNDS declining > 75bp over 6 months), 20-day forward yield rises in more than 50% of cases, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS change and yield change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in Industrial Production exceeding 0.5% signals robust economic activity, raising real rate expectations and lifting the 10-year yield over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After INDPRO >0.5% m/m triggers, the 5-day forward yield rises less than 60% of the time, or the average yield change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in total nonfarm payrolls from its current level signals weakening labor demand that precedes a drop in aggregate consumption, reducing growth expectations and lowering the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months following a negative PAYEMS print, the 20-day forward yield falls less than 55% of the time, or the median yield change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 3% and retail sales growth turns negative year-over-year, the combination of monetary accommodation and collapsing consumer demand confirms a deep recession scenario that drives a steep decline in the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both triggers are true, the 20-day forward yield falls less than 65% of the time, or the average yield change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP over the most recent four quarters raises aggregate demand and inflation expectations, lifting the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling correlation between GDPC1 4-quarter growth and 60-day forward yield change is below 0.05, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.
